raw.title: Reinforced Masonry Wall — ASD
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse 🖨 Print / PDF ⇓ Save ⇑ Load Reinforced Masonry Wall & Parapet — ASD 8″, 10″, 12″ CMU · Single / Double Reinforced · Partial / Full Grout · TMS 402-22 / MDG-2022 TMS 402-22 MDG-2022 Ch. 11 §8.3.4 Axial & Flexure §8.3.5 Shear §8.4 Deflection ASCE/SEI 7-22 🧱 Wall (Pin–Pin) 🏗 Parapet (Cantilever) → Import from other ARE calculators: 📺 Open C&C Wind Calc ▶ Load C&C from Memory 📺 Open Diaphragm Calc ▶ Load Diaphragm from Memory ASD Method (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8): This calculator uses Allowable Stress Design. All loads are entered at service level (unfactored) — the calculator does not apply load factors internally. For strength design (LRFD slender wall per Ch. 9), use Enercalc or a comparable program. ASD and LRFD will give different numerical results; both are valid per TMS 402-22. WALL SECTION — OUT-OF-PLANE LOADING As (tension) w = 20.0 psf P h = 12.0 ft t = 7.63″ d = 3.81″ EXT. INT. Wall Section — Out-of-Plane CMU PLAN VIEW (LOOKING DOWN) grout grout As EXTERIOR FACE INTERIOR FACE t = 7.63″ s = 32″ o.c. | 7 cells @ 8″ = 56″ CMU Plan View (Looking Down) IN-PLANE SHEAR — WALL ELEVATION V = 500 plf R Lw = 20.0 ft h = 12.0 ft Vert. @ 32″ Horiz. @ 48″ In-Plane Shear — Wall Elevation ● Wall Configuration CMU Size & Reinforcement Nominal Wall Size 8″ CMU (t = 7.625″) 10″ CMU (t = 9.625″) 12″ CMU (t = 11.625″) Grout Condition Partially Grouted (rebar cells only) Fully Grouted Reinforcement Type Single Reinforced (tension side only) Double Reinforced (tension + compression) ℹ 8″ CMU: t = 7.625″, tfs = 1.25″ | An (partial) = 30.0 in²/ft | An (full) = 91.5 in²/ft | Suggested d (mid-depth) = 3.81″ ● Material Properties TMS 402-22 §4.2 Masonry Type CMU (concrete masonry) Clay brick masonry f′m — Specified Compressive Strength psi 1,500 psi 2,000 psi 2,500 psi 3,000 psi 3,500 psi Steel Grade Grade 60 (Fs = 32,000 psi) Grade 40/50 (Fs = 20,000 psi) ℹ Em = 900 × 2000 = 1800000 psi | n = 16.11 | Fb = 0.45 × 2000 = 900 psi | Fs = 32000 psi ● Wall Geometry, Axial Loads & Reinforcement IBC ASD Combos Design Span h ft Unsupported height for slenderness & OOP moment Wall Self-Weight — Dead Load, e = 0 (centered on wall): Wall Weight psf Weight per sf of wall face area Total Wall Height ft Full height above design point (can exceed design span for parapet, upper floors) Pwall plf 660 = Weight × Height | Applied at wall centroid, no eccentricity Applied Axial Loads — with eccentricity (from bearing, ledger, joist seat, etc.): Dead PD plf Live PL plf Roof Live PLr plf Snow PS plf Wind PW plf Vertical component (+dn / −uplift) Eccentricity e in From wall CL to load line of action ▶ Governing IBC ASD Combo: D + 0.6W | Ptotal = 660 plf | includes OOP wind | Unity ≈ 0.095 Vertical (Out-of-Plane) Reinforcement — Tension Side: OOP Bar Size #4 (0.20 in²) #5 (0.31 in²) #6 (0.44 in²) #7 (0.60 in²) #8 (0.79 in²) #9 (1.00 in²) OOP Bar Spacing s in 8″ 16″ 24″ 32″ 40″ 48″ 56″ C-C spacing of vertical bars (8″ cell grid) Effective Depth d in From comp. face to tension steel CL. Suggested: 3.81″ (mid-depth). ● Out-of-Plane Loading OOP Wind / Seismic — Service Level Service Wind Pressure w psf (unfactored — ASD) Net OOP design pressure at service level. For C&C wind: use the direct zone pressure (do not multiply by load factors). Import from C&C Calc above. OOP Moment Calculation Simply Supported: M = wh²/8 Cantilever (fixed base): M = wh²/2 Fixed-Fixed (top & base): M = wh²/12 Manual Moment Input ● In-Plane Shear Shear Wall Demand — Service Level Unit Shear v plf V/Lw in plf. Import from Diaphragm Calc above. Wall Length Lw ft Length of the shear wall segment M / (V · dv) Ratio Clamped to [0.25, 1.0] per TMS 402-22 §8.3.5 Horizontal (In-Plane Shear) Reinforcement: Horiz. Bar Size None (Fvs = 0) #4 (0.20 in²) #5 (0.31 in²) #6 (0.44 in²) Horiz. Bar Spacing s in Vertical spacing between horizontal bars ▶ Run All Checks ✔ PASS — Max D/C = 0.332 8″ CMU | partial grout | Single Reinforced | f′m = 2000 psi | Grade 60 | h = 12.0ft | w = 20.0 psf (service) ⇄ ResultsGov. axial demand: 660 lb/ft→Total OOP moment: 360 lb-ft/ftIn-plane shear (full wall): 10 kips→Allowable axial Pa: 18,319 lb/ftOOP deflection: 0.02 inMax D/C ratio: 0.33 Section & Material Properties Property Value Wall size / actual thickness 8″ nominal, t = 7.63″ Face shell tfs 1.25″ Grout Partially Grouted An (net area) 30.0 in²/ft In (net inertia) 308.7 in&sup4;/ft r (radius of gyration) 3.208″ h/r slenderness 44.89 ≤ 99 (Eq. 8-16) Em 1800000 psi n = Es/Em 16.11 Fb = 0.45f′m 900.0 psi Fs 32000 psi γg (shear grout factor) 0.7 As vertical #5 @ 32.0″ = 0.116 in²/ft, d = 3.81″ Av horizontal #4 @ 48.0″ = 0.050 in²/ft Applied Demands (Service Level) Demand Value Gov. IBC ASD Combo D + 0.6W (w/ wind) Pwall (self-weight) 660 plf (55 psf × 12.0 ft, e = 0) Ptotal (governing) 660 lb/ft OOP wind w 20.0 psf (service, unfactored) Mwind 360.00 lb·ft/ft Mecc = Papp×e 0.00 lb·ft/ft Mtotal OOP 360.00 lb·ft/ft Voop 120.0 lb/ft In-plane unit shear v 500 plf In-plane total V 10.00 kips (Lw = 20.0ft) Deflection δ / allowed 0.017″ / 0.960″ 660 lb/ft Axial Ptotal D + 0.6W 360.00 lb·ft/ft Total OOP Moment 217.9 psi / 900.0 psi fb / Fb (masonry) 10633.3 psi / 32000 psi fs / Fs (steel) 500 plf In-Plane Unit Shear 16.67 psi / 54.00 psi fv / Fv (in-plane) 0.017″ OOP Deflection 0.332 Max D/C Ratio Code Checks — ASD (TMS 402-22) Check Reference Demand Allowable D/C Status Detail Axial Compression h/r = 44.89 | R = 0.897 | D + 0.6W TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2 P = 660 lb/ft Pa = 18319 lb/ft 0.036 PASS ► Calc OOP Flexure — Masonry k = 0.248 | j = 0.9173 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fb = 217.9 psi Fb = 900.0 psi 0.242 PASS ► Calc OOP Flexure — Steel As = 0.116 in²/ft | ρ = 0.0025 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs = 10633.3 psi Fs = 32000 psi 0.332 PASS ► Calc Combined Axial + Flexure fa/Fa = 0.036 | fb/Fb = 0.242 TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.1 fa/Fa + fb/Fb ≤ 1.0 0.278 PASS ► Calc OOP Shear V = 120.0 lb/ft | γg = 0.7 TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.2 fv = 4.00 psi Fv = 39.07 psi 0.102 PASS ► Calc In-Plane Shear Fvm=55.81 | Fvs=21.33 psi TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.1 fv = 16.67 psi Fv = 54.00 psi 0.309 PASS ► Calc OOP Deflection Ieff=308.7 in&sup4;/ft | Icr=18.7 in&sup4;/ft TMS 402-22 §8.4 δ = 0.017″ h/150 = 0.960″ 0.017 PASS ► Calc Engineering Notes: ASD vs. Strength Design: This calculator uses ASD (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8). Enercalc's "Masonry Slender Wall" uses Strength Design (Ch. 9) with factored loads and φ factors — different method, not a bug. For the same wall geometry, ASD and LRFD typically give different numerical results; verify the appropriate method with the project EOR. All service loads are entered unfactored. The calculator applies no load factors internally. Deflection uses Branson's effective moment of inertia (Ieff). The TMS Ch. 9 Slender Wall method uses Icr directly, yielding larger deflections. Verify δallow per ASCE 7 or project specs (h/150 used here). Axial allowable Pa includes steel contribution (0.65AstFs) per Eq. 8-16/17. Eccentricity e adds moment Me = P×e/12 to wind moment for combined OOP demand. In-plane Fvs derived per-foot using dv = 0.8×12 = 9.6 in (unit-width basis). For full-wall check verify dv = 0.8Lw. Partial grout γg = 0.70 reduces allowable shear per TMS 402-22 §8.3.5. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub× Max D/C ratio0.332 Reinforced Masonry Wall — ASD · 7/11/2026 Copy OOP deflection0.017 in Reinforced
